cases.label: anomaly_envelope_e27aab7b
cases.authoritativeBaselineDescription:
The following architectural room envelope was extracted from the baseline image.
This description is considered authoritative.
Do not reinterpret the baseline architecture from the staged image.
Determine whether the staged image still represents this same architectural room envelope.

Room overview:
- camera orientation: unknown
- visible wall planes: front / camera-facing wall
- extracted reference openings: 2
- extracted architectural landmarks: 1
- ceiling continuity: treat as continuous unless the staged image clearly shows a changed boundary junction
- floor continuity: treat as continuous unless the staged image clearly shows a changed boundary junction

left wall: continuous wall plane with no extracted openings or fixed architectural anchors
rear wall: continuous wall plane with no extracted openings or fixed architectural anchors
right wall: continuous wall plane with no extracted openings or fixed architectural anchors
front / camera-facing wall: reference openings: A1: walkthrough opening, center third, full height, occupancy 10-20, confidence 1.00; D1: door, left third, full height, occupancy 20-40, confidence 1.00 | fixed landmarks: AC1: built-in AC unit, center third, confidence 1.00

Architectural landmarks:
- AC1: built-in AC unit, front / camera-facing wall, center third, confidence 1.00

Opening reference points (context only, do not validate opening preservation or resize):
- A1: walkthrough opening, front / camera-facing wall, center third, full height, occupancy 10-20, confidence 1.00
- D1: door, front / camera-facing wall, left third, full height, occupancy 20-40, confidence 1.00

Use the baseline description as the source of truth.
Do not rebuild the room envelope from the staged image.
Do not reinterpret the baseline architecture from scratch.
The envelope validator owns only the permanent wall envelope.
Architectural openings may be used only as reference points for understanding wall continuity.
Do not evaluate whether an opening itself has changed.
Do not evaluate fixtures, flooring, or furniture except where they provide architectural context for understanding wall continuity.
cases.fullyInterpolatedPrompt:
You are validating whether two images represent the exact same physical room architecture.

Compare the BASELINE image and the STAGED image.

GLOBAL RULE
Furniture, decor, staging objects, and movable items may change.
Only the permanent architectural wall envelope must remain the same.

BASELINE ENVELOPE DESCRIPTION:
The following architectural room envelope was extracted from the baseline image.
This description is considered authoritative.
Do not reinterpret the baseline architecture from the staged image.
Determine whether the staged image still represents this same architectural room envelope.

Room overview:
- camera orientation: unknown
- visible wall planes: front / camera-facing wall
- extracted reference openings: 2
- extracted architectural landmarks: 1
- ceiling continuity: treat as continuous unless the staged image clearly shows a changed boundary junction
- floor continuity: treat as continuous unless the staged image clearly shows a changed boundary junction

left wall: continuous wall plane with no extracted openings or fixed architectural anchors
rear wall: continuous wall plane with no extracted openings or fixed architectural anchors
right wall: continuous wall plane with no extracted openings or fixed architectural anchors
front / camera-facing wall: reference openings: A1: walkthrough opening, center third, full height, occupancy 10-20, confidence 1.00; D1: door, left third, full height, occupancy 20-40, confidence 1.00 | fixed landmarks: AC1: built-in AC unit, center third, confidence 1.00

Architectural landmarks:
- AC1: built-in AC unit, front / camera-facing wall, center third, confidence 1.00

Opening reference points (context only, do not validate opening preservation or resize):
- A1: walkthrough opening, front / camera-facing wall, center third, full height, occupancy 10-20, confidence 1.00
- D1: door, front / camera-facing wall, left third, full height, occupancy 20-40, confidence 1.00

Use the baseline description as the source of truth.
Do not rebuild the room envelope from the staged image.
Do not reinterpret the baseline architecture from scratch.
The envelope validator owns only the permanent wall envelope.
Architectural openings may be used only as reference points for understanding wall continuity.
Do not evaluate whether an opening itself has changed.
Do not evaluate fixtures, flooring, or furniture except where they provide architectural context for understanding wall continuity.

Use the baseline description as the source of truth.
Do not rebuild the room envelope from the staged image.
Do not reinterpret the baseline architecture from scratch.
The envelope validator owns only the permanent wall envelope.
Architectural openings may be used only as reference points for understanding wall continuity.
Do not evaluate whether an opening itself has changed.
Do not evaluate fixtures, flooring, or furniture except where they provide architectural context for understanding wall continuity.

Return ok=false if the staged image shows any of these architectural changes:
* newly created wall sections or wall planes
* walls extended into former open space
* room corners moved, appeared, or disappeared
* recesses, alcoves, bulkheads, soffits, or wall offsets filled or flattened
* wall planes continuing where the baseline showed open space or a recess
* new wall appearing at the edge of the frame
* ceiling, floor, or boundary junctions that no longer match the baseline envelope
* structural columns or fixed architectural supports added, removed, or relocated

This is not a furniture question.
Furniture, curtains, rugs, plants, artwork, lighting, appliances, and other staging objects are allowed if the architectural envelope remains the same.

Do not treat a foreground object as an envelope change unless it is actually replacing or extending the architectural envelope.
Do not treat perspective or crop variation as structural change unless the same envelope can no longer be identified.

If the baseline shows a recess, corner, gap, or wall interruption and the staged image shows a continuous opaque wall surface in that same area, return ok=false.

If a new opaque wall surface appears where the baseline had open space, treat that as architectural modification, not staging.

If the geometry is ambiguous, prefer visual ambiguity over claiming a structural change without clear evidence.

Return JSON only:

{
  "ok":true|false,
  "reason":"short explanation",
  "confidence":0.0-1.0,
  "boundaryLinesMissing": true|false,
  "continuousSurfaceReplacement": true|false,
  "noPlausibleVisualExplanation": true|false,
  "visualAmbiguity": true|false,
  "reasonCode": "envelope_visual_ambiguity"|"envelope_insufficient_geometric_evidence"|"envelope_confirmed_structural_change"
}

ARCHITECTURAL CERTAINTY RULE (MANDATORY)
Set ok=false only when the staged image clearly shows the architectural envelope has changed.

For structural certainty, all must be true:
1) boundaryLinesMissing
2) continuousSurfaceReplacement
3) noPlausibleVisualExplanation

Non-fail certainty guard:
- If recess/offset is faint but partially visible, do not claim certainty.
- If edges are reduced but not eliminated, do not claim certainty.
- If lighting, shadow loss, or smoothing can explain flattening, do not claim certainty.
- If geometry is ambiguous, set visualAmbiguity=true and do not claim certainty.
cases.semanticReasoning: The architectural wall envelope, including all specified openings and landmarks, remains identical between the baseline and staged images. No structural changes, new wall sections, or altered room geometry are observed.
